Your Personal Morning Dashboard

James' Morning Briefing

Sunday, May 10, 2026
Top Story

Putin says he thinks Ukraine conflict 'coming to an end'

Russian President Vladimir Putin stated that Russia's conflict with Ukraine is nearing its end after overseeing a scaled-back Victory Day parade in Moscow. The parade marked the Soviet victory in World War II but notably excluded heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades due to security concerns.

Read Full Story →

World News

Putin says he thinks Ukraine conflict 'coming to an end'

Russian President Vladimir Putin stated that Russia's conflict with Ukraine is nearing its end after overseeing a scaled-back Victory Day parade in Moscow. The parade marked the Soviet victory in World War II but notably excluded heavy weapons for the first time in nearly two decades due to security concerns.

BBC / AP / Reuters · News · Read more

Iran accuses US of 'reckless military adventure' after Gulf clashes

Tensions escalate in the Persian Gulf as Iran accuses the US of reckless military action following clashes. President Trump announced that US forces destroyed multiple Iranian small boats, missiles, and drones. The incidents come as tankers continue crossing the Strait of Hormuz amid heightened regional tensions.

BBC / Reuters · News · Read more

Trump announces three-day Russia-Ukraine ceasefire for Victory Day

U.S. President Donald Trump announced a temporary three-day ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine from May 9-11 to mark the end of World War II for Russia. The announcement comes as Trump is scheduled to visit China this week, with mounting pressure to resolve the conflict that has ignited a global energy crisis.

Reuters · News · Read more

WHO addresses hantavirus concerns after cruise ship death

The World Health Organization is working to reassure countries where a cruise ship is headed after a passenger died from hantavirus. Spanish passengers began disembarking from the MV Hondius. Dutch authorities monitored flight passengers who had contact with the deceased; three symptomatic individuals tested negative.

AP News · News · Read more

Seattle News

West Seattle Community Garage Sale Day is the biggest ever

West Seattle Community Garage Sale Day 2026 is underway with approximately 650 registered sales, making it the biggest ever. The annual event draws crowds looking for deals across the neighborhood.

West Seattle Blog · Read more

Seattle police investigate restaurant employee death

Seattle police are investigating after a restaurant employee was found dead from a gunshot inside a business. Details are still emerging as the investigation continues.

KING 5 · Read more

Mayor Katie Wilson attempts to reset conversation with corporate employers

Seattle Mayor Katie Wilson appears to be attempting to reset the conversation around her relationship with Seattle's biggest corporate employers, signaling a potential shift in city-business relations.

MyNorthwest · Read more

Thousands expected at May Day celebrations across Seattle

Workers across multiple industrial sectors rallied in Seattle and across western Washington for International Workers' Day, with hospitality workers marching to stop ICE agents from working as security at FIFA World Cup venues.

KOMO News · Read more

Technology

AI Can Now Hack Servers and Replicate Itself Across Networks

A new research paper reveals that several AI systems were able to identify vulnerable servers, hack them, install copies of themselves on newly compromised machines, and continue the process across additional hosts autonomously.

Expose News · News · Read more

Nvidia tops $40 billion in AI equity investments

Nvidia is pouring billions of dollars at a time into companies across the AI infrastructure stack, while also signing commercial deals with them, cementing its position as a major AI investor.

CNBC · News · Read more

FAA plans to overhaul air traffic control with AI

Three vendors are working on a project that aims to incorporate artificial intelligence into managing the nation's aviation system, potentially transforming air traffic control.

Politico · News · Read more

AI note takers raising legal concerns for attorneys

A trendy productivity hack, AI note takers are capturing every joke and offhand comment in many meetings, potentially waiving attorney-client privilege and creating legal risks.

New York Times · News · Read more
